name: Frías and Ebro Natural Way Loop
name_original: Pueblo de Frías — circular por el Río Ebro
description: Jog the moderate 9.1-mile Frías and Ebro Natural Way Loop, featuring medieval architecture, river views, and 502 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Jogging routes Frías traverse a landscape shaped by the Ebro River and the distinctive La Muela Hill, offering varied terrain for runners. The region is characterized by its position within the Tobalina Valley, framed by the Montes Obarenes Natural Park, which provides diverse natural features and elevation changes. Running here involves paths along riverbanks, ascents and descents around the town's unique geological setting, and trails through the broader natural park. Best jogging routes around Frías The most popular jogging route is Frías and Ebro Natural Way Loop, a 9.1 miles (14.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 39 minutes to complete. This route follows the Ebro River, offering views of the surrounding natural environment. Another top favourite among local runners is PRC-BU 95 Portillo del Busto Trail, a difficult 6.1 miles (9.8 km) path. This trail features significant elevation changes, providing a challenging experience through varied terrain. Local runners also love the Frías Castle – Frías Medieval Bridge loop from Frías, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through the historic town center and past its medieval bridge, often completed in about 52 minutes. Jogging around Frías is defined by the Ebro River banks, the unique La Muela Hill, and the diverse landscapes of the Montes Obarenes Natural Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate river loops to more difficult trails with considerable elevation gain. The routes in Frías are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 90 runners have used komoot to explore Frías's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Frías?
A: Frías offers a good selection of running routes, with over 20 trails available for exploration. These routes cater to various fitness levels, ranging from moderate riverside paths to more challenging trails with significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Frías?
A: The running trails in Frías feature diverse terrain. You can expect paths along the banks of the &lt;strong&gt;Ebro River&lt;/strong&gt;, ascents and descents around the unique &lt;strong&gt;La Muela Hill&lt;/strong&gt; where the town is perched, and trails through the varied landscapes of the &lt;strong&gt;Montes Obarenes Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;. This mix provides both flat sections and challenging inclines.

Q: Are there running routes that pass by waterfalls in Frías?
A: Yes, the nearby village of Tobera, part of the Frías municipality, is known for the waterfalls of the Molinar River. While not a dedicated running route, you can incorporate a visit to the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3929492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobera Waterfalls and Medieval Bridge&lt;/a&gt; into a shorter run or cool-down walk for a refreshing experience.

Q: Can I find running routes with historical landmarks or viewpoints in Frías?
A: Absolutely. Frías is rich in history. You can enjoy a run that takes you through the historic town center and past its medieval bridge on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39560095" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frías Castle – Frías Medieval Bridge loop from Frías&lt;/a&gt;. Other highlights in the area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4987028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Saint Mary of La Hoz&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4986755" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tobera Waterfalls Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering scenic breaks during your run.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Frías?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Frías are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051260180/frias-and-ebro-natural-way-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frías and Ebro Natural Way Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic circular path along the Ebro River.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of running trails in Frías?
A: The running trails around Frías offer a range of difficulty levels. You'll find moderate routes, often along riverbanks, and more difficult trails with considerable elevation gain, especially those venturing into the Montes Obarenes Natural Park. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1051250339/prc-bu-95-portillo-del-busto-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PRC-BU 95 Portillo del Busto Trail&lt;/a&gt; is considered difficult due to its significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Frías?
A: The running routes in Frías are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Runners often praise the high landscape values, the diversity of the terrain, and the opportunity to combine physical activity with exploring the region's unique natural and historical features.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running trails in Frías?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, routes with less elevation and shorter distances, such as sections along the Ebro River, can be suitable for families. The scenic paths around Tobera Waterfalls also offer a pleasant, shorter option for a walk or light jog with children.

Q: Are the running trails in Frías dog-friendly?
A: Generally, trails in natural parks and rural areas like Frías are dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage and regulations, particularly within the Montes Obarenes Natural Park, to ensure a pleasant experience for both you and your pet.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Frías?
A: Frías offers a compelling environment for outdoor activities throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide the most comfortable temperatures for running, with pleasant weather and vibrant natural scenery. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially in the mornings or evenings, while winter offers a unique, quieter experience, though some higher elevation trails might be more challenging.

Q: Is there parking available near the running trails in Frías?
A: Yes, as a town that attracts visitors, Frías typically has parking options available, especially near the town center or main access points to popular trails. It's advisable to check specific starting points for routes for the most convenient parking.

Q: Can I access running routes in Frías using public transport?
A: Frías is a smaller town, and while it may have some public transport connections to larger cities in the Burgos province, direct access to specific trailheads via public transport might be limited. It's best to research local bus schedules if you plan to rely on public transport to reach the area.
tips: Impressive fortress that rises above the medieval town of Frías. Its imposing silhouette and walls evoke centuries of history and provide a panoramic view of the region. This castle, declared an Asset of Cultural Interest, is an architectural treasure that invites you to immerse yourself in the past and enjoy its beauty and grandeur.
Alongside the bridge is a great place for a swim.
Frías is one of the most beautiful towns in Burgos as it is located at the top of a hill. The church and a large number of houses rise and can be seen on your arrival.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Frías and Ebro Natural Way Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific trailhead parking for this exact loop isn't detailed, the medieval town of Frías is the natural starting point. You'll likely find parking options within or just outside the town, which provides easy access to the route along the Ebro River.

faqs.question: Is the Frías and Ebro Natural Way Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally suitable for beginners. While the tour is rated as moderate due to its distance and some elevation changes, many sections of the wider Ebro Nature Trail around Frías are described as easily-accessible paths. Joggers with basic fitness should find it enjoyable.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You can expect a mix of surfaces, including asphalted roads and numerous dirt tracks. Around Frías, many paths are noted as easily-accessible, making for a generally runnable surface. Some sections might involve slight inclines or declines as you navigate the riverbanks and surrounding areas.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience picturesque and varied scenery. The route offers views of the Ebro River, riparian forests, and the charming medieval village of Frías with its historic Frías Castle and unique 'hanging houses'. You'll also pass by the impressive Frías Bridge, all set within a landscape shaped by the river and its valleys.

faqs.question: What historical landmarks or natural features can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in both natural beauty and historical significance. Key highlights include the stunning Frías Castle, the medieval Frías Bridge, and the entire town of Frías itself, perched dramatically on a hill. You'll also enjoy the natural features of the Ebro River, Spain's second-longest, and its surrounding riparian forests.

faqs.question: Is the Frías and Ebro Natural Way Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Frías and Ebro Natural Way Loop is generally dog-friendly. Many natural trails in the area welcome dogs. However, always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, especially when passing through populated areas or near wildlife, and remember to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay an entrance fee to jog the Frías and Ebro Natural Way Loop. It's a public route accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this route is typically during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and comfortable. Summers can be hot, especially in the middle of the day, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable then. Winters can be chilly, but the trail remains accessible, offering a different, quieter experience.

faqs.question: Does the Frías and Ebro Natural Way Loop connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other official trails. You'll find it covers significant sections of the Camino natural de Ebro - Castilla y León, and also touches upon parts of the Canal de Fontecha, Ruta de los Monteros del Rey, and Ruta de los Sentidos, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the town of Frías, which serves as the starting and ending point for this loop, offers various options for cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel and relax after your jog.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for jogging this route?
faqs.answer: For jogging this route, wear comfortable athletic clothing suitable for the weather conditions. Good running shoes with decent grip are recommended for the mixed terrain. Don't forget to bring water, especially on warmer days, and perhaps a light snack. Sunscreen and a hat are also advisable during sunny periods.
